data.title: Bybit CEO has started a live broadcast to respond to the details of the ETH theft incident
Costa Rica's Banco Nacional Introduces Bitcoin ETF
data.description:
According to CoinDesk, Banco Nacional (BN), Costa Rica's largest commercial bank, is set to launch a spot bitcoin exchange-traded fund (ETF) through its investment management division, BN Fondos. This initiative marks a significant milestone as it provides Costa Ricans with their first opportunity to invest in cryptocurrency products via the national banking system. Alongside the bitcoin ETF, BN Fondos is also introducing an S&P 500 ETF, with a minimum investment requirement of $100 for each fund. These investments will be conducted in U.S. dollars rather than Costa Rican colones.

Pablo Montes de Oca, the general manager at BN Fondos, explained that while Costa Rican regulations do not recognize bitcoin as an investment vehicle, the ETF structure allows for such investments. Banco Nacional, which serves over 2.1 million customers, representing more than 40% of Costa Rica's population, is pioneering this move in the region. Despite the absence of formal cryptocurrency laws in Costa Rica, the country's constitution and civil code permit activities not explicitly prohibited by law, allowing Costa Ricans to trade and own cryptocurrencies.

In 2022, a comprehensive crypto regulation bill known as the Crypto Asset Market Law was introduced in the Legislative Assembly. However, it stalled at the commission level. The proposed legislation aimed to formalize the use of cryptocurrencies for transactions involving goods and services without granting any cryptocurrency, including bitcoin, the status of legal tender. This development underscores the evolving landscape of cryptocurrency regulation in Costa Rica, as the nation navigates the complexities of integrating digital assets into its financial system.

data.title: Bybit Faces $700 Million in Net Outflows Following Massive Hack, DefiLlama Founder Reports
data.description: The aftermath of the Bybit hack continues to unfold, as DefiLlama founder 0xngmi revealed that user withdrawals have resulted in a net outflow of $700 million as of February 22. The staggering outflows highlight the growing concerns among traders and investors following the $1.4 billion exploit of Bybit’s ETH multi-signature cold wallet.

data.title: Bybit Update: Bybit CEO Goes Live to Address $1.44 Billion ETH Theft Incident
data.description: In response to the massive $1.44 billion security breach, Bybit CEO Ben Zhou has launched a live broadcast on the Bybit platform, providing further insights into the ETH asset theft that has rocked the exchange.According to previous reports, Bybit's multi-signature cold wallet was compromised, leading to unauthorized withdrawals of over 500,000 ETH-related assets, including:401,347 ETH ($1.12 billion)90,376 stETH ($253.16 million)15,000 cmETH ($44.13 million)8,000 mETH ($23 million)With hackers already dispersing 490,000 ETH to 49 addresses and an additional 15,000 cmETH in the unstaking process, concerns over fund recovery and security upgrades remain at the forefront.

data.title: Bybit Hack: Bybit Hackers Move 490,000 ETH Across 49 Addresses, 15,000 cmETH Still Pending Unstake
data.description: The Bybit exploit, which resulted in the theft of 514,000 ETH worth $1.429 billion, has seen the hackers disperse 490,000 ETH across 49 different addresses, with each address receiving 10,000 ETH, according to blockchain monitoring firm EmberCN.In addition to the massive ETH transfers, the attackers are also attempting to unstake 15,000 cmETH, a move that requires an 8-hour waiting period before funds become available. The possibility of intercepting the pending unstake remains uncertain at this stage.

data.title: Ethena's unrealized profit and loss in Bybit has dropped to $10 million. USDDe has gradually returned to its anchor after a short-term breakout below $0.98.
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Ethena Labs published a post on X stating that the previously mentioned $30 million unrealized profit and loss on the Bybit platform has been reduced to $10 million, and Bybit's unrealized profit and loss exposure is expected to be zero in the next hour. Currently, the liquidity of stablecoins supporting USDe is $2 billion, and users can redeem it immediately if they wish.
On-chain data shows that after the Bybit theft, USDe once de-anchored and fell below $0.98 in the short term, and has now gradually returned to the anchor, temporarily reporting $0.9972.

data.title: Safe: We are working with Bybit to investigate, and the wallet has temporarily suspended some functions
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Safe posted on the X platform that the security team is working with Bybit to investigate and has not yet found evidence that the official Safe front-end has been hacked. However, out of caution, Safe Wallet has temporarily suspended some functions. User safety is the top priority and more updates will be provided soon.

data.title: Defillama founder: After the theft, Bybit has had a net outflow of $700 million due to user withdrawals
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Defillama founder 0xngmi posted on X that since the theft, Bybit has seen a net outflow of $700 million due to user withdrawals.

data.title: Bybit Hack: SlowMist Unveils Technical Details Behind Bybit’s $1.4 Billion Hack
data.description: Blockchain security firm SlowMist has disclosed a detailed breakdown of the attack that led to Bybit’s massive Ethereum (ETH) breach, revealing how the hackers manipulated smart contract logic to gain control over the exchange’s cold wallet.According to SlowMist’s investigation, the exploit began with the deployment of a malicious implementation contract on February 19, 2025, at 7:15:23 UTC (0xbDd077f651EBe7f7b3cE16fe5F2b025BE2969516). The attacker strategically leveraged multisig ownership, using three owner signatures to replace the legitimate Safe implementation contract with the malicious one at 14:13:35 UTC on February 21, 2025 (0x46deef0f52e3a983b67abf4714448a41dd7ffd6d32d32da69d62081c68ad7882).The core of the attack relied on embedding malicious upgrade logic into STORAGE via DELEGATECALL[0x0] (0x96221423681A6d52E184D440a8eFCEbB105C7242). This backdoor allowed the hacker to execute sweepETH and sweepERC20 functions, effectively draining the targeted wallet’s ETH and ERC-20 token holdings.Bybit has assured users that its other cold wallets remain unaffected and that withdrawals are operating normally. However, industry leaders—including Binance executives Yi He and former CEO CZ—have stepped in, offering support in tracking the stolen funds and strengthening security measures.The incident underscores the persistent vulnerabilities in crypto exchange security and the growing sophistication of attacks targeting digital asset custodians.

data.title: SlowMist releases Bybit attacker's operation details
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News SlowMist released the details of the Bybit attacker's operation: 
1) A malicious implementation contract was deployed at UTC 2025-02-19 7:15:23: https://etherscan.io/address/0xbdd077f651ebe7f7b3ce16fe5f2b025be2969516 
2) UTC 2025-02-21 14:13:35, the attacker used three owners to sign a transaction and replaced Safe's implementation contract with a malicious contract: https://etherscan.io/tx/0x46deef0f52e3a983b67abf4714448a41dd7ffd6d32d32da69d62081c68ad7882
3) The attacker then used the backdoor functions “sweepETH” and “sweepERC20” in the malicious contract to steal the hot wallet.
